meta.title: Genesis Mission: Q1 2026 Interactive Status Report
meta.summary: Launched in late 2025, the Genesis Mission is a comprehensive initiative to rapidly modernize the U.S. energy infrastructure. Track progress, funding distribution, and the projected path to a 100% carbon-free grid by 2035.

subtitle: Department of Energy • Status Report
description: Launched in late 2025, the Genesis Mission is a comprehensive initiative to rapidly modernize the U.S. energy infrastructure. This interactive dashboard provides a real-time overview of our progress, funding distribution, and the projected path to a 100% carbon-free grid by 2035.

timeline.label: Q4 2025
timeline.title: Mission Launch & Architecture
timeline.description: The Genesis Mission was officially enacted. Initial focus was on establishing the technical architecture, finalizing the budget allocation frameworks, and opening the portal for state-level proposals.

timeline.label: Q1 2026
timeline.title: Initial Seed Funding & Deployment
timeline.description: Currently, the DOE is actively distributing the first $15 Billion tranche of the total budget. Focus areas include finalizing state-level grid modernization grants and initiating AI-driven energy load balancing pilots in high-demand metropolitan areas.

milestones.text: Initial funds disbursed: 45% of Q1 target
AI Pilot programs initiated in 3 major cities

timeline.label: Q3 2027
timeline.title: Grid Prototypes & SMR Groundbreaking
timeline.description: By late 2027, the first next-generation smart grids will go live. Additionally, groundbreaking will commence for the first commercially viable Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) funded by the initiative.

timeline.title: The 50% Clean Energy Milestone
timeline.description: The critical midpoint of the Genesis Mission. Success is defined by achieving a 50% reduction in carbon emissions compared to 2005 levels, heavily reliant on the scaling of completed 2026–2028 projects.

budgetAllocation.description: Upgrading transmission lines, implementing AI-driven load balancing, and increasing physical grid resilience against extreme weather events.
Funding research, regulatory streamlining, and initial construction of Small Modular Reactors to provide stable, carbon-free base load power.
Accelerating next-gen solar efficiency, offshore wind deployment, and crucial investments in utility-scale solid-state battery storage.
Grants and training programs to transition fossil-fuel workers into high-paying roles within the clean energy sector.

projectionNotes.title: Coal Phase-out
projectionNotes.description: Targeting a complete cessation of unmitigated coal power generation by 2030, replaced primarily by wind and solar arrays built on former industrial sites.

projectionNotes.title: Nuclear Renaissance
projectionNotes.description: Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) are projected to account for 30% of base-load generation by 2035, providing stable, carbon-free power regardless of weather.

projectionNotes.title: Renewable Dominance
projectionNotes.description: Solar, Wind, and Hydro are expected to collectively provide 60% of the grid's power, supported by massive investments in solid-state battery storage.
